claim: Collatz dossier: Tao (2019, revised 2022) proved that almost all Collatz orbits in logarithmic density attain values below any divergent comparison function; Barina (2020) reported convergence verification below 2^68; a four-cycle relay should target bounded residue-certificate formalization rather than a global proof claim.

Goldbach dossier: Oliveira e Silva, Herzog, and Pardi (2014) verified strong Goldbach up to 4 * 10^18; Helfgott (2013) proved ternary Goldbach; Chen (1973) proved the prime plus at-most-two-prime-factors theorem; the recommended relay target is a bounded witness certificate formalizable in Lean.

BSD dossier: modularity over Q plus Gross-Zagier (1986) and Kolyvagin (1989) give rank-0/rank-1 frontier results for modular elliptic curves over Q, while the recommended relay target is a selected-curve numerical BSD quotient audit plus Lean-certified finite-field local data.

Ranked recommendation: commit Relay Cycle 1 resources first to strong Goldbach, second to Collatz, third to BSD, because Goldbach offers the narrowest falsifiable certificate path and the fastest route to a formalized bounded theorem.
